user_prompt: A user requested deletion of all their data. Generate the DSAR-deletion checklist including which systems to scrub.
actual_output:
## DSAR Deletion / Erasure Checklist — GDPR Articles 12, 17, 19, 28

*Compliance guidance only — not legal advice. Confirm edge cases with legal counsel or your DPO/privacy lead, especially where retention laws, disputes, fraud prevention, or financial records apply.*

### 1. Intake and Verification

| Step | Action | GDPR Reference |
|---|---|---|
| Log request | Record date received, channel, requester identity, request type: deletion/erasure | Art. 12(2), 12(3) |
| Verify identity | Use proportionate verification. Do not collect excessive new data | Art. 5(1)(c), Art. 12(6) |
| Confirm scope | Determine whether the user requests deletion of all account data, marketing data, support history, analytics identifiers, etc. | Art. 12, Art. 17 |
| Start deadline clock | Respond without undue delay and within 1 month unless extension is justified | Art. 12(3) |
| Check user location/status | Confirm if GDPR applies, but consider honoring deletion globally as best practice | Best practice |

---

### 2. Assess Whether Erasure Applies

Under **GDPR Article 17**, deletion is required where one of these applies:

- Data is no longer necessary for the original purpose.
- User withdraws consent and no other lawful basis applies.
- User objects to processing under legitimate interests and there are no overriding grounds.
- Data was unlawfully processed.
- Deletion is required by EU or Member State law.
- Data was collected from a child in relation to information society services.

### 3. Check for Lawful Retention Exceptions

Before deleting, identify data that must or may be retained under **Article 17(3)**.

| Possible retention reason | Example | Action |
|---|---|---|
| Legal obligation | Tax, accounting, invoicing, AML/KYC, employment records | Restrict processing and retain only required fields |
| Establishment, exercise, or defense of legal claims | Active dispute, chargeback, fraud investigation, litigation hold | Preserve necessary evidence only |
| Freedom of expression/information | User-generated public content in limited contexts | Legal review recommended |
| Public interest, archiving, research, statistics | Usually uncommon for SaaS/customer data | Legal/DPO review required |
| Security/fraud prevention | Abuse logs, ban records, fraud indicators | Retain minimized data if justified under Art. 6 and Art. 17(3) |

**Important:** If any data is retained, document the lawful basis, retention period, access restrictions, and reason for refusal or partial refusal.

---

## 4. Systems to Scrub or Review

Use this as a system-by-system deletion checklist.

### A. Core Product and Account Systems

- [ ] User account database  
- [ ] User profile table  
- [ ] Authentication provider  
- [ ] Single sign-on provider  
- [ ] Identity management system  
- [ ] Password reset records  
- [ ] Session tokens  
- [ ] Refresh tokens  
- [ ] API keys linked to the user  
- [ ] Device records  
- [ ] User preferences/settings  
- [ ] Saved addresses/contact details  
- [ ] Uploaded files or documents  
- [ ] User-generated content  
- [ ] Comments, posts, messages, reviews, or forum entries  
- [ ] In-app notifications  
- [ ] App-specific metadata and configuration records  

**Action:** Delete, anonymize, or detach personal identifiers where total deletion would break system integrity.

---

### B. Customer Relationship and Sales Systems

- [ ] CRM contact record  
- [ ] Lead records  
- [ ] Account/contact associations  
- [ ] Sales notes  
- [ ] Demo request forms  
- [ ] Webinar/event registrations  
- [ ] Contact enrichment data  
- [ ] Call recordings/transcripts  
- [ ] Sales email sequences  
- [ ] Pipeline/opportunity records containing personal data  

**Action:** Delete contact data unless needed for contract, legal claim, or financial record retention.

---

### C. Marketing and Communications

- [ ] Email marketing platform  
- [ ] Newsletter lists  
- [ ] Suppression lists  
- [ ] Consent management platform  
- [ ] Cookie consent records  
- [ ] Marketing automation platform  
- [ ] SMS/push notification tools  
- [ ] Referral or affiliate systems  
- [ ] Survey tools  
- [ ] NPS/customer feedback platforms  
- [ ] Ad audience platforms  
- [ ] Retargeting/remarketing audiences  
- [ ] Lookalike/similar audience seeds  
- [ ] Lead magnets/download records  
- [ ] UTM/campaign attribution records linked to the user  

**Note:** You may need to retain a minimal suppression record, such as hashed email, to ensure the user is not re-added to marketing lists. This should be documented under legitimate interests or legal obligation, depending on jurisdiction and applicable ePrivacy rules.

---

### D. Billing, Payments, and Finance

- [ ] Billing system  
- [ ] Payment processor customer record  
- [ ] Subscription management platform  
- [ ] Invoice records  
- [ ] Receipts  
- [ ] Refund records  
- [ ] Chargeback records  
- [ ] Tax/VAT records  
- [ ] Accounting software  
- [ ] Expense/reimbursement records if applicable  
- [ ] Accounts receivable notes  

**Action:** Do not delete records required for tax, accounting, fraud prevention, or legal claims. Instead:

- Minimize personal data.
- Restrict access.
- Mark as “erasure requested — retained for legal obligation.”
- Apply retention schedule.
- Delete after statutory retention expires.

Relevant GDPR basis: **Art. 17(3)(b), Art. 6(1)(c), Art. 5(1)(e)**.

---

### E. Support and Customer Success

- [ ] Helpdesk tickets  
- [ ] Support chat logs  
- [ ] Support email inboxes  
- [ ] Customer success notes  
- [ ] Call recordings  
- [ ] Screen recordings  
- [ ] Bug reports submitted by user  
- [ ] Feature requests  
- [ ] Community/forum support posts  
- [ ] Knowledge base comments  
- [ ] Escalation records  
- [ ] Incident reports referencing the user  

**Action:** Delete or anonymize unless retention is required for legal claims, service integrity, or security investigation.

---

### F. Analytics, Product Telemetry, and Tracking

- [ ] Web analytics user profile  
- [ ] Product analytics platform  
- [ ] Session replay tools  
- [ ] Heatmap tools  
- [ ] A/B testing platforms  
- [ ] Event tracking database  
- [ ] Data warehouse  
- [ ] BI dashboards  
- [ ] Attribution platform  
- [ ] Mobile analytics SDKs  
- [ ] Crash reporting tools  
- [ ] Error monitoring tools  
- [ ] Logs containing user IDs, email addresses, IP addresses, device IDs, or session IDs  
- [ ] Pseudonymous identifiers linked to user account  
- [ ] Data lake raw event streams  

**Action:** Delete user-level analytics and identifiers. Aggregate anonymous metrics may be retained if they cannot reasonably identify the individual.

Relevant GDPR basis: **Art. 5(1)(c), Art. 5(1)(e), Art. 17**.

---

### G. Security, Logs, and Infrastructure

- [ ] Application logs  
- [ ] Web server logs  
- [ ] CDN logs  
- [ ] WAF/firewall logs  
- [ ] Authentication logs  
- [ ] Audit trails  
- [ ] Admin activity logs  
- [ ] IP access logs  
- [ ] SIEM/security monitoring platform  
- [ ] Intrusion detection/prevention systems  
- [ ] Abuse/fraud monitoring tools  
- [ ] Backup indexes  
- [ ] Cloud storage buckets  
- [ ] Search indexes  
- [ ] Cache stores  
- [ ] Message queues  
- [ ] Email delivery logs  
- [ ] Webhook logs  
- [ ] API gateway logs  

**Action:** Security logs may be retained for a limited period if needed for security, fraud prevention, or legal claims. Apply strict retention, access controls, and minimization. Do not use retained logs for marketing or product analytics.

---

### H. Backups and Disaster Recovery

- [ ] Database backups  
- [ ] File backups  
- [ ] Object storage snapshots  
- [ ] VM/container snapshots  
- [ ] Disaster recovery replicas  
- [ ] Offline backup archives  

**Recommended approach:**

- Do not normally restore backups solely to delete one person unless technically feasible and proportionate.
- Ensure deleted data is not reintroduced into production if backups are restored.
- Maintain a deletion tombstone/suppression record.
- Apply backup expiration aligned with your retention schedule.
- Document backup deletion constraints and expected purge date.

Relevant GDPR considerations: **Art. 17, Art. 25, Art. 32**.

---

### I. Third-Party Processors and Sub-Processors

Under **GDPR Article 28**, processors must assist with deletion requests.

- [ ] Hosting provider  
- [ ] Cloud infrastructure provider  
- [ ] Email service provider  
- [ ] CRM provider  
- [ ] Marketing automation provider  
- [ ] Analytics provider  
- [ ] Payment processor  
- [ ] Support/helpdesk provider  
- [ ] Survey provider  
- [ ] Data enrichment provider  
- [ ] Identity/authentication provider  
- [ ] Logging/monitoring provider  
- [ ] Customer communication/chat provider  
- [ ] Data warehouse/BI provider  
- [ ] Backup/DR provider  

**Action checklist:**

- [ ] Identify all processors holding the user’s personal data.
- [ ] Submit deletion request through vendor DSAR/deletion tools.
- [ ] Confirm completion or scheduled deletion.
- [ ] Record vendor response date.
- [ ] Confirm any retained data and legal basis.
- [ ] Verify DPA includes deletion assistance obligations under Art. 28(3)(e), 28(3)(g).

---

## 5. Deletion Execution Record

Create an internal audit trail without retaining unnecessary personal data.

| Field | Record |
|---|---|
| DSAR case ID | Internal reference |
| Request received date | Date |
| Identity verified | Yes/No |
| Verification method | Minimal description |
| Scope of request | Full deletion / partial deletion |
| Systems searched | List |
| Data deleted | Categories, not excessive detail |
| Data anonymized | Categories |
| Data retained | Categories and reason |
| Legal basis for retention | Art. 17(3), Art. 6 basis |
| Processors notified | List |
| Processor completion evidence | Ticket/reference IDs |
| Response sent date | Date |
| Final deadline | Date |
| Reviewer | Privacy lead/DPO/legal |

---

## 6. User Response Template

Subject: Confirmation of Your Data Deletion Request

Hello,

We have processed your request to delete your personal data.

We have deleted or anonymized personal data associated with your account from systems where erasure is required under applicable data protection law.

Certain limited records may be retained where necessary for legal, accounting, security, fraud prevention, or dispute-resolution purposes. These records are restricted, retained only for the applicable retention period, and not used for marketing or non-essential purposes.

Where your data was held by third-party service providers acting on our behalf, we have instructed them to delete or return the relevant data where required.

If you have questions about this response, or wish to challenge the outcome, you may contact us at: [privacy contact email].

You may also have the right to lodge a complaint with your local data protection supervisory authority.

Regards,  
[Company Privacy Team]

---

## 7. Internal Completion Criteria

Mark the DSAR deletion request complete only when:

- [ ] Identity has been verified proportionately.
- [ ] All relevant systems have been searched.
- [ ] Data has been deleted, anonymized, or lawfully retained.
- [ ] All processors have been notified where applicable.
- [ ] Retained data has a documented Article 17(3) exception.
- [ ] Suppression/tombstone record is created if needed.
- [ ] Backups are covered by documented expiry and restore procedures.
- [ ] The user has been informed within the GDPR deadline.
- [ ] DSAR case file is retained under a defined retention period.
- [ ] Any systemic issues are logged for remediation.

---

## 8. Recommended Remediation if Checklist Cannot Be Completed

If you cannot confidently identify all places the user’s data exists, this indicates broader GDPR gaps:

| Gap | GDPR Risk | Remediation |
|---|---|---|
| No data inventory | Art. 30, Art. 5(2) accountability | Build record of processing activities |
| No deletion workflow | Art. 12, Art. 17 | Implement DSAR SOP |
| No processor map | Art. 28 | Create vendor inventory and DPA tracker |
| No retention schedule | Art. 5(1)(e) | Define retention periods by data category |
| Unsearchable logs/data lake | Art. 25, Art. 32 | Implement searchable identifiers or minimization |
| No backup deletion policy | Art. 17, Art. 25 | Add backup expiry and restore suppression controls |
judge_reasoning: The chatbot provided a comprehensive and detailed DSAR-deletion checklist, including specific systems to scrub and relevant GDPR citations, fully addressing the user's request.
